title: Microsoft CEO has a message for Google, OpenAI, Anthropic and all the AI companies
description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has a pointed message for AI giants OpenAI, Anthropic and Google: you can't warn that AI will wipe out jobs, call your own models a weapon, and then demand unlimited money and data centers. In a Wall Street Journal interview, Nadella criticised how a few frontier labs are capturing AI's value, pushed cheaper models and DeepSeek as alternatives, and said the industry must still earn society's "social permission."

description: Former South Africa batter Daryll Cullinan expresses concern over the physical toll on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive batting style.  Cullinan fears for the youngster's wrists and joints, citing the increased demands of modern cricket and heavier bats. He draws parallels to Sachin Tendulkar's career-threatening elbow injury, urging attention to Sooryavanshi's long-term health as his workload grows.

title: Resort charged Rs 15 extra on a Mountain Dew bottle, court orders Rs 10k compensation
description: A Haryana resort faces a consumer commission's order to refund Rs 75 and pay Rs 10,000 in compensation. The resort overcharged a customer for Mountain Dew during a party, charging Rs 50 per bottle against a printed price of Rs 35. When the customer raised the issue, resort staff allegedly misbehaved. The commission found this a clear deficiency in service, ordering the refund with interest and compensation for harassment.

This move signifies deepening strategic ties and India's growing prowess as a defence exporter. The UAE's interest stems from a desire to bolster its military capabilities amidst regional security concerns, marking a potential major win for India's indigenous defence industry.

Jaipur woman held under UAPA over links to Pakistan-based terror group
description: Rajasthan ATS apprehended a woman in Jaipur for alleged ties with Pakistan-based Jaish-e-Mohammed and other anti-national elements. Investigations revealed her social media accounts and phone contained objectionable content and links to foreign profiles, including those displaying extremist symbols. She reportedly converted to Islam after guidance from a Pakistan-based Mufti and was allegedly being prepared for travel to Pakistan. Booked under UAPA, she is in ATS custody.

title: Several dead after massive blaze breaks out at Lucknow coaching centre
description: Tragedy struck Lucknow's Aliganj area as a devastating fire engulfed a coaching-cum-gaming institute, leaving four feared dead and several injured. Panic ensued as the blaze spread rapidly, trapping individuals inside. Authorities launched a massive rescue operation, with the chief minister directing immediate on-site supervision and ensuring aid for victims. An investigation into the cause and safety lapses is underway.

'Speed up your calculation': Gukesh advised ahead of World Championship match
description: Ahead of his world title defence against Javokhir Sindarov, D Gukesh has been advised by Grandmaster Krishnan Sasikiran to improve his speed, technique and handling of time pressure. Sasikiran warned that Sindarov is highly prepared and fast-paced, but said Gukesh's previous world championship experience could prove crucial in what promises to be a tough contest.

title: WC: Messi's Argentina, Mbappe's France in focus as final dash for knockouts begins
description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 is entering a thrilling group stage finale with 42 nations vying for 29 spots in the Round of 32. Only USA, Mexico, and Germany have secured early qualification, while Haiti, Turkiye, and Tunisia are out. Powerhouses like Argentina, France, England, and Portugal are still fighting for advancement. The week promises intense competition and simultaneous matches as the knockout picture takes shape.

title: 'Maa Inti Bangaram' BO Day 4 [LIVE]: Samantha Ruth Prabhu film nets Rs 24.72 crore
description: Samantha Ruth Prabhu's 'Maa Inti Bangaram' delivered a rare feat over its opening weekend, three consecutive days of growth rather than the usual weekend plateau. After a Rs. 5.35 crore Day 1, the film jumped 43% on Day 2 to Rs. 7.65 crore and a further 32% on Day 3 to Rs. 10.10 crore, taking its three-day domestic net to Rs. 23.10 crore and worldwide gross to Rs 41.79 crore. As the film enters its first Monday, live tracking shows Rs. 0.82 crore collected so far across 1,236 shows, split Rs 0.77 crore in Telugu and Rs. 0.05 crore in Tamil, pushing the cumulative domestic net to Rs. 23.92 crore. With momentum building with each day, B.V. Nandini Reddy's female-led action-family drama looks well positioned for a strong first week.

title: Quote of the day by Taylor Swift
description: Taylor Swift, a pop music icon, redefined 'fearless' in her 2008 album 'Fearless'. She believes true bravery isn't the absence of fear, but acting despite it. This philosophy, evident in her lyrics about love, high school, and perseverance, highlights her enduring impact. Swift, now a billionaire, continues to inspire with her candid approach to life's challenges.

description: West Bengal's new BJP government has unveiled its inaugural budget, prioritizing welfare and employment. Key announcements include a significant Dearness Allowance hike for state employees, the creation of one lakh government jobs, and a substantial Rs 36,000 crore direct cash transfer scheme for women. The budget also proposes infrastructure development and support for various worker categories, aiming to restore fiscal discipline and public trust.

title: Expert Explains | Why Keir Starmer resigned as British Prime Minister and leader of the Labour Party
Starmer steps down as UK PM: From internal revolts to lost confidence, 5 reasons behind the exit
description: Keir Starmer has resigned as Labour leader, citing a need for change after significant election setbacks and internal party dissent. Concerns over his ability to win the next general election, coupled with senior figures openly challenging his leadership and a perceived failure to deliver on 2024 promises, have led to his departure. Scandals also contributed to the pressure for new leadership.

title: Road built on their land without permission, owners get justice after 40 years
description: The Supreme Court has fined the Chhattisgarh government Rs 2 lakh for illegally occupying private land for nearly four decades without compensation. The court upheld the high court's order, awarding landowners enhanced compensation and interest from the date they first filed their eviction suit in 2006. Justices deemed the state's appeal frivolous, aimed at harassing the landowners.

A notable exception was Nashik, where a BJP rebel secured victory, causing a setback for the Shiv Sena.

What happens to a flat when the owner dies without a will?
description: Without a will, property inheritance in India involves intricate legalities that vary by religion. The Hindu Succession Act, Muslim Personal Law, and Indian Succession Act determine the distribution of assets among heirs. Grasping these laws, along with understanding the classifications of heirs and their shares, is vital for the efficient transfer of property. Essential documents, such as legal heir certificates and relinquishment deeds, play a pivotal role in this process.

The fee, intended to curb foreign worker replacement of Americans, was struck down by a judge citing separation of powers. DHS contends the President has authority for such measures, warning that delays allow more foreign workers entry, potentially causing irreparable damage.

description: The registration window for SSC CGL 2026 will close at 11 PM on June 22, giving aspirants their final opportunity to apply for one of the country’s largest government recruitment drives. The examination aims to fill around 12,256 vacancies across central government departments. Candidates can pay the application fee until June 23, while the correction facility will remain available from June 29 to July 1.

This remarkable performance, which saw India A clinch the title, served as a powerful response to a previous 'not IPL' jibe.  Sooryavanshi's fearless batting has solidified his reputation as a prodigious talent.

Man rapes and murders 4-year-old to settle scores with her kin over UP panchayat poll rivalry
description: In a tragic incident in Kasganj district, a four-year-old girl was kidnapped, raped, and murdered, allegedly as an act of revenge linked to a past panchayat election conflict. She was reported missing on June 6, with her body found three days later. Authorities have made one arrest and are pursuing the primary suspect, who allegedly confessed to the brutal crime.

How Delhi's new slum rehabilitation policy aims to reshape housing for families
description: Delhi's new slum policy, approved by Union Home Minister Amit Shah, aims for large-scale redevelopment of informal settlements through public-private partnerships.  The "Jahan Jhuggi Wahi Makan" initiative will provide permanent housing and essential civic infrastructure to an estimated four lakh families, with an extended eligibility cut-off of January 1, 2025. This ambitious urban renewal project seeks to unlock land value for financing and create integrated neighbourhoods.

description: Chhattisgarh is emerging as a crucial transit point for a sophisticated interstate drug trafficking network. Recent seizures reveal traffickers are constantly evolving their methods, using hidden compartments and fake plates to move cannabis from Odisha to northern and western India. Police are targeting the "white-collar drug mafia" operating under legitimate business fronts, with a recent bust uncovering a Rs 4.5 crore racket.

Royal Ascot: Where the real spectacle isn't the horses
description: For over 300 years, Royal Ascot has been one of Britain's grandest social events. But on screen, the horses are often little more than supporting cast. The real drama unfolds in the stands — in the hats, the hierarchy and the silent performance of belonging.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
description: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

title: ‘Start counting your days, principal ji’: Bihar kid’s school threat goes viral
description: A young student from Bihar's dramatic reaction to being scolded by his teacher has taken the internet by storm. The boy's bold declaration, "Will destroy your school!" has gone viral, sparking widespread amusement and comparisons to movie villains. This unfiltered display of childhood emotion resonated with many, reminding adults of their own dramatic youthful outbursts and providing a refreshing dose of simple, unintentional comedy.

title: At Royal Ascot, India arrives in couture, fascinators and nine yards
description: From royal pageantry and thoroughbred racing to handloom pride and Bollywood glamour, Royal Ascot has increasingly become a stage where Indian identity travels with elegance.

title: Enjoy spicy food? Here's what psychology says about you
